seo.description: Building the Earth in Minecraft - Join the world's largest community project to recreate our planet the video-game Minecraft.

title.default: Builiding the Earth in Minecraft 1:1 | BuildTheEarth

seo.title: Building The Earth In Minecraft
seo.description: Join BuildTheEarth and help recreate the planet in Minecraft at 1:1 scale.

image.alt: Roundabout in a french city, background image

whoWeAre.title: Who we are
whoWeAre.description: BuildTheEarth is a community project creating a complete 1:1 scale replicate of every building on Earth in the computer game Minecraft, a virtual copy of our whole world that showcases the diversity of culture and living space on our planet and stands as a testament for what we can achieve when we work together as humanity. Together we connect, share and learn from builders worldwide, and explore Earth like never before. Join our global community and help bring our world to life!

globalCommunity.title: Our global Community
globalCommunity.description: Each region or country is built on a Minecraft server and the progress is managed by subteams of BuildTheEarth. To start building, search for the region's Team and see how you can help them out! Many people start by building their own neighborhood or even just their own house and then they go on to build more. Don't be scared that you are not good enough to build. We all start with something small. Remember that we are there to help you learn how to build more complicated things.

explore.title: Explore the Earth
explore.description: All of BuildTheEarth's progress is free to view and explore for visitors. The progress is separated onto different servers depending on where in the world it is. All you have to do is find the IP of the region you want to explore and connect to it! Many servers can be connected to though our central hub accessible from buildtheearth.net on Java edition.

howToHelp.title: How you can help
howToHelp.description: To start building, search for the region's team and see how you can help them out! All Teams require you to "apply" which is aimed at teaching you the basics of building on the Earth world. This usually consists of recreating 1 or 2 small buildings on their server. You can always ask for help and building advice on our Discord server. Experienced builders are always more than willing to help you get started.

notfound.title: Not found...
notfound.description: The page you are trying to open does not exist. You may have mistyped the address, or the page has been moved to another URL.

seo.title: Build Teams
seo.description: Explore BuildTheEarth teams, find your region, and join a Minecraft server to start contributing.

teams.title: Build Teams
description: BuildTheEarth is divided into subteams, which build specific countries or areas of the world.
Each Team has its own Minecraft server, where you can join and start building.

tooltip.location: A list of countries this team builds in

seo.notFoundDescription: The Build Team you are looking for does not exist.

ownPage.noRecentImages: {team} has not uploaded any images yet.

joinServer.label: Join the server
step1: Select Minecraft Java Edition on the left sidebar.
Click the green <launcherButton>PLAY</launcherButton> button to start Minecraft.
Once Minecraft has started, click <button>Multiplayer</button> on the main menu.
joinServer.tip: For the best experience you may need to change your game version to {version}.
step2: Click <button>Add Server</button> on the bottom right.
For Server Name enter a label for yourself (like {name}).
Click the server you just added in the list.
joinServer.done: Done! You are now on the {name} Minecraft Server.

locationsList.label: Locations
locationsList.description: {buildTeam} is building in multiple countires. You can find the full list of countries below. If you want to view this list on a map, click the button below.

seo.title: Frequently Asked Questions
seo.description: Get answers to common BuildTheEarth questions, including joining teams, building rules, and community details.

differentLanguageAlert.title: Translation of Frequently asked Questions
differentLanguageAlert.description: All questions listed on this page are written and answered in English. If you need help with getting an answer, please message us on Discord.

seo.title: Contact Us
seo.description: Reach BuildTheEarth through social channels, staff contacts, or appeals support.

appeals.title: Ban Appeals
appeals.description: If you wish to appeal your ban on our Discord server, please send an E-Mail to <mail></mail>. In order to submit a ban appeal, you must include your Discord name and ID, screenshot of your ban message and your reason for appealing. Illegitimate appeals will be ignored.

seo.title: Get Started
seo.description: Learn how to explore servers, find your team, and begin building with BuildTheEarth.

arrowDown.alt: Scroll down to learn more about how to get started

explore.title: Explore BuildTheEarth
explore.description: Discover the incredible progress made by our global community as they recreate the world.

content.text:
Depending on the country which you would like to visit, there are multiple servers available. You can search for a country in the box below.



Otherwise, you can also explore our progress by checking out the gallery or visiting our map page.

joinServer.title: Explore {country}
joinServer.description:
Join the {name} Minecraft server to explore the amazing builds from this region.



If you need help navigating the server, dont hesitate to reach out to us on Discord!

build.title: Start Building
build.description: Join us and contribute to the creation of a 1:1 scale replica of Earth in Minecraft.

content.text:
BuildTheEarth is organized into multiple Build Teams, each responsible for recreating different regions of the world. Build Teams often focus on specific countries or continents, allowing you to contribute to areas you are passionate about.



There are no restrictions on which or how many Build Teams you can join, so feel free to explore how many as you like!



Each Build Team has its own server where you can collaborate with other builders, participate in events, and get support. Due to this, there is no single "application process".

Instead, simply join the Discord server of the Build Team you are interested in and follow their simple instructions to get started.



You can also join our Hub Server (Minecraft: buildtheearth.net, Discord) to get help and meet other builders from around the world!

joinServer.title: Build in {country}
joinServer.description:
Join the {name} Minecraft server to help build in this region!



Dont forget to join the Discord Servers with the links below!

seo.title: Outreach
seo.description: Read media coverage, press features, and outreach stories about BuildTheEarth.

contact.title: Media Inquiries
contact.description: For media inquiries, partnership opportunities, or any other outreach-related questions, please contact us at <mail></mail>. We look forward to hearing from you! You can find us on various <contact>social media platforms</contact> as well.

seo.title: Our Progress
seo.description: Discover what Build The Earth has achieved so far and where we are headed next.

meta.title: Our Progress
meta.description: Discover what Build The Earth has achieved so far and where we are headed next.

achievements.title: What We Have Achieved So Far
achievements.description: Our measured completion may look modest at first glance, but this project was never about speed-running a percentage. It is about building global collaboration, preserving local identity, and creating a lasting digital record of our world together.

progress.title: Progress Estimates
progress.description: These metrics show our progress as a fraction of Earth's total area, a humbling reminder that the mission is about quality and community, not speed.

builders.label: Builders
builders.description: People actively collaborating in teams, mentoring newcomers, and sharing techniques worldwide.

buildings.label: Buildings Tracked
buildings.description: A growing archive of real-world structures recreated with care, research, and local knowledge.

area.label: Area Covered
area.description: Large and small regions mapped into claims so teams can coordinate work across continents.

claimCompletion.title: Claim Completion
claimCompletion.description: {finishedClaims} of {activeClaims} active claims are marked finished.

landCoverage.title: Earth Land Mass Coverage
landCoverage.description: Progress ({trackedAreaKm2} km²) of Earth's land surface.

totalCoverage.title: Earth Total Area Coverage
totalCoverage.description: Progress ({trackedAreaKm2} km²) of Earth's total surface.

impact.title: Low Percentages. Massive Impact.
impact.description: BTE is not a race to 100%. We are building cities with historical context, local detail, and thousands of volunteer hours. Even a single district done right can educate, inspire, and connect people across countries.

seo.title: Showcase Gallery
seo.description: Explore the BuildTheEarth showcase gallery with community builds from around the world.

seo.title: Blog
seo.description: Read BuildTheEarth news, updates, and stories from the global community.

blog.intro: Ever wonder how we build our projects, what goes on behind the scenes, or want to stay updated with our latest news? Then this is the place! Dive into a world of creativity, innovation, and community spirit as we share stories, updates, and insights from our journey to recreate Earth in Minecraft.
